meta.title: Inversion & Emphasis
meta.description: Learn formal inversion structures and cleft sentences for emphasis.
meta.description_ko: 강조를 위한 격식체 도치 구조와 분열문을 학습합니다.

explanation.en: After negative adverbs (never, seldom, rarely), invert subject and auxiliary: 'Never have I seen...'
explanation.ko: 부정 부사(never, seldom, rarely) 다음에는 주어와 조동사를 도치합니다: 'Never have I seen...'

questions.prompt: 부정 부사(Never, Seldom, Rarely 등) 뒤에 오는 어순은?

explanation.en: 'Never have I seen' — inversion after 'never' with present perfect.
explanation.ko: 'Never have I seen' — 현재 완료와 함께 'never' 뒤에 도치.

questions.prompt: Never _____ such a beautiful sunset before. (나는 이렇게 아름다운 석양을 본 적이 없습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Not only' at the start of a clause triggers inversion: 'Not only did + subject + verb'.
explanation.ko: 절의 시작에 'not only'가 오면 도치가 발생합니다: 'Not only did + 주어 + 동사'.

questions.prompt: 'Not only did she win the race, but she also broke the record.' — 도치가 일어난 이유는?

explanation.en: 'Seldom' at the start triggers inversion: 'Seldom have I seen'.
explanation.ko: 'seldom'이 문두에 오면 도치가 발생: 'Seldom have I seen'.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Seldom have I seen such dedication.

explanation.en: 'Only after + clause/noun' triggers inversion: 'did I understand'.
explanation.ko: 'only after + 절/명사' 다음에는 도치가 발생합니다.

questions.prompt: Only after the meeting _____ the full extent of the problem. (회의가 끝난 후에야 문제의 전체 범위를 깨달았습니다.)

explanation.en: 'It was John who...' emphasises 'John' as the person who did the action.
explanation.ko: 'It was John who...'는 행동을 한 사람으로 'John'을 강조합니다.

questions.prompt: 분열문(cleft sentence) 'It was John who broke the window.'의 강조 대상은?

explanation.en: 'It was + noun phrase + that' = cleft sentence emphasising the place.
explanation.ko: 'It was + 명사구 + that' = 장소를 강조하는 분열문.

questions.prompt: It was _____ that she first realised her passion for music. (그녀가 음악에 대한 열정을 처음 깨달은 것은 대학에서였습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Hardly + past perfect inversion' (had I arrived) + when + past simple.
explanation.ko: 'Hardly + 과거완료 도치'(had I arrived) + when + 단순 과거.

questions.prompt: 'Hardly had I arrived when it started to rain.' — 이 문장의 특징은?

explanation.en: 'Not only did + subject + verb, but also + clause' — double achievement structure.
explanation.ko: 'not only did + 주어 + 동사, but also + 절' — 이중 성취 구조.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Not only did she win the gold medal but she set a new world record.

explanation.en: 'What I need is...' — pseudo-cleft sentence. 'What clause + is + complement'.
explanation.ko: 'What I need is...' — 의사 분열문. 'What 절 + is + 보어'.

questions.prompt: What I need _____ a good night's sleep. (내가 필요한 것은 충분한 수면입니다.)

explanation.en: After 'rarely', inversion is required: 'Rarely does she go...'
explanation.ko: 'rarely' 다음에는 도치가 필요합니다: 'Rarely does she go...'

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'Rarely she goes out alone at night.'
choices: Rarely she goes → Rarely does she go

explanation.en: 'Under no circumstances + inversion' is a strong negative prohibition.
explanation.ko: 'under no circumstances + 도치'는 강한 부정적 금지를 나타냅니다.

questions.prompt: 'Under no circumstances should you reveal the password.' — 이 문장의 특징은?
choices: 부정 전치사구가 문두에 와서 도치 발생

explanation.en: 'Only after + noun/clause' triggers inversion in the following clause.
explanation.ko: 'only after + 명사/절' 다음 절에서 도치가 발생합니다.

questions.prompt: _____ the financial crisis did people realise how fragile the economy was. (금융 위기 이후에야 사람들은 경제가 얼마나 취약한지 깨달았습니다.)

explanation.en: Cleft sentence emphasising the event: 'It was winning the lottery that...'
explanation.ko: 사건을 강조하는 분열문: 'It was winning the lottery that...'

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: It was winning the lottery that changed his life.

explanation.en: 'No sooner + past perfect inversion + than + past simple': 'No sooner had she arrived than...'
explanation.ko: 'No sooner + 과거완료 도치 + than + 단순 과거': 'No sooner had she arrived than...'

questions.prompt: No sooner _____ arrived than the argument began. (그녀가 도착하자마자 논쟁이 시작됐습니다.)

explanation.en: 'So + adjective + be + subject' is an inversion structure for emphasis.
explanation.ko: 'So + 형용사 + be + 주어'는 강조를 위한 도치 구조입니다.

questions.prompt: 'So successful was the campaign that it was copied worldwide.' — 'so' 뒤에 도치가 일어난 이유는?

explanation.en: 'What I love is + infinitive' — pseudo-cleft for emphasis.
explanation.ko: 'What I love is + 부정사' — 강조를 위한 의사 분열문.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: What I love is to travel to new places.

explanation.en: 'At no point + inversion': 'At no point did we compromise...'
explanation.ko: 'At no point + 도치': 'At no point did we compromise...'

questions.prompt: At no point _____ our commitment to quality. (우리는 품질에 대한 헌신을 타협한 적이 없습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Little did I know' = I had no idea. 'Little' + inversion for strong negative emphasis.
explanation.ko: 'Little did I know' = 전혀 몰랐다. 'little' + 도치로 강한 부정적 강조.

questions.prompt: 'Little did I know that she was the CEO.' — 이 문장의 의미는?
choices: 나는 그녀에 대해 조금 알고 있었다
그녀가 CEO인 것을 전혀 알지 못했다

explanation.en: 'It is + adjective + that' = cleft-like structure for emphasis.
explanation.ko: 'It is + 형용사 + that' = 강조를 위한 분열문 유사 구조.

questions.prompt: It is _____ that she speaks six languages fluently. (그녀가 6개 국어를 유창하게 구사한다는 것이 놀랍습니다.)

explanation.en: 'Not until + clause/phrase + inversion': 'Not until the results were published did we know'.
explanation.ko: 'not until + 절/구 + 도치': 'Not until the results were published did we know'.

questions.prompt: 틀린 부분을 찾으세요: 'Not until the results were published we knew the truth.'

explanation.en: 'Such + be + subject + that' — inversion with 'such' for emphasis.
explanation.ko: 'Such + be + 주어 + that' — 강조를 위한 'such'를 사용한 도치.

questions.prompt: 다음 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하세요.
questions.answer: Such was her talent that she received many offers.

explanation.en: 'Although' clauses do not trigger inversion — they are subordinate clauses.
explanation.ko: 'although' 절은 도치를 유발하지 않습니다 — 종속절입니다.

questions.prompt: 다음 중 도치가 필요하지 않은 문장은?
choices: Never have I heard such nonsense.
Although she was tired, she continued working.

explanation.en: 'Neither have I' agrees with a negative statement in present perfect.
explanation.ko: 'Neither have I'는 현재 완료의 부정 진술에 동의합니다.

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: I've never seen such a talented musician. B: _____ I. She's extraordinary.'
